findings.title: CORS wildcard on admin API exposes credentials to any origin
findings.evidence: The diff replaces a strict single-origin allowlist `["https://admin.example.com"]` with the wildcard `"*"`. Any web page on any domain can now issue cross-origin requests to this admin API. Combined with any session-cookie or Authorization-header auth scheme, this enables cross-site request forgery and credential leakage. The Diff Drift report flags this as high severity with the same evidence.

notes: Only one file changed and it is fully analyzed — no skipped-file ambiguity.
The wildcard value is a plain string `"*"`, not a dynamic reflect-origin function, so there is no conditional path that could restrict it at runtime based on this diff alone.
A fix should restore the explicit allowlist or introduce an environment-variable-driven allowlist rather than using a wildcard.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
